input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

## 1. `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource`

`StaticResource` resolves **once** at load time. To react to runtime theme changes, use `DynamicResource`:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryColor}" />

<!-- ✓ updates when the dictionary changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryColor}" />
```

Then swap themes by merging the new dictionary at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDictionaries = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDictionaries.Clear();
mergedDictionaries.Add(new DarkTheme());   // or LightTheme
```

## 2. Activity restart on system theme change

Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` on a `uiMode` config change. Prevent it by adding `ConfigChanges` to your `MainActivity`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.UiMode
        | ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Orientation)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This tells Android your app handles the change itself, so it won't restart. You can then detect the system toggle with `Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged` and swap your dictionaries accordingly.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two .NET MAUI issues related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind theme colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and doesn't update when the resource dictionary changes. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the Android system theme changes, the Activity is recreated by default. They need to handle the configuration change to prevent the restart.

Let me give a concise, helpful answer.
